_comment: Detection signatures for harness capabilities: how to tell, from a session transcript, that a
capability ACTUALLY FIRED rather than merely being registered and deployed.
Why this file exists. Three states get conflated and each one hides a different failure:
registered  listed in manifest.source.json — means nothing at runtime
deployed    the file is where the agent would look for it
fired       something actually caused it to run
Rules sat registered and deployed for months without ever loading. code-verifier is deployed and
named in review-gate form 4, and unverified claims still went out repeatedly. A capability nothing
observes is a capability nobody can tell is broken.
Signature kinds:
skill    a Skill tool_use whose skill name matches `match`
bash     a Bash tool_use whose command matches the `match` regex
text     assistant output matching the `match` regex (proves a RESULT was produced, not just that
a tool ran — the strongest evidence, and the only kind available for prompt-level rules)
state    a file on disk whose presence proves the capability ran (hooks leave these)
`enforcement` records how the capability is compelled to run, which is what decides whether a miss is
a defect or a judgment call:
hook      a hook blocks or acts; a miss is a defect
advisory  the model chooses; a miss may be legitimate under rules/native-capability-first
Adding a capability is an edit here, not a code change.

Signature precision matters more than coverage. A pattern that matches a file being COMPILED, LINTED or merely NAMED reports a capability as fired when it never ran, which is worse than no detector at all: it manufactures confidence. Require the subcommand to follow the script, anchor with \b, and remember that alternation binds the WHOLE pattern — 'a b|c' means '(a b)' or '(c)', not 'a (b|c)'.

capabilities.label: Enforced code review
capabilities.expect_when: any turn that changed a code file

capabilities.label: Round task document + Stop gate
capabilities.expect_when: a round with more than about ten sub-tasks is open

capabilities.label: Fake-run detection before a completion claim
capabilities.expect_when: the turn claims tests pass, code works, or a result was produced

capabilities.label: Six-question audit of a research claim
capabilities.expect_when: the turn states a comparative or empirical result

capabilities.label: Document preferences + doccheck linter
capabilities.expect_when: the turn writes or edits a human-facing document

capabilities.label: Reusable prompt corpus
capabilities.expect_when: a request resembles a scenario already in the library

capabilities.label: Privacy scan before publishing
capabilities.expect_when: the turn publishes, commits, or shares a document

capabilities.label: Harness mismatch filed for later repair
capabilities.expect_when: a harness feature was skipped because native ability was better

capabilities.label: Registered rules verified reachable
capabilities.expect_when: rules were added, or a new agent was set up

capabilities.label: Turn ends with an explicit state marker
